[METADATA/INTRO]: GALVESTON, Texas -- The flames could be seen for miles Saturday night as Galveston firefighters responded to a large grass fire on East Beach. It started some time before 8 p.m....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: GALVESTON, Texas -- The flames could be seen for miles Saturday night as Galveston firefighters responded to a large grass fire on East Beach. 
 It started some time before 8 p.m. 
 Social media photos showed large flames coming near Seawall Boulevard. 
 No injuries were reported.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2022-01-10
[TITLE]: Another Climate Catastrophe: the Boulder, Colorado Fire - Climate Adaptation Center
[METADATA/INTRO]: Climate Warming Spawns Another Disaster Everyone is aware of the apocalyptic fire near Boulder Colorado on December 30, 2021, the second climate-related billon-dollar loss in December, following the unprecedented December 12th tornado outbreak in the Mid-South. (See our post “Historic...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Climate warming, in short, set up the disaster you are about to see. 
 The cities of Superior and Louisville turned to ashes as 100-mph winds and bone-dry conditions set off a firestorm. 
 Boulder weather radar showed the smoke plume heading eastward in the high winds, carrying burning chunks of wood and debris downwind, expanding the fire. 
 MAXAR Aerospace, a company I’ve worked with, provided this next satellite image. The top panel shows a pre-fire image. The bottom panel is a late afternoon image of smoke and fire. The bright areas are actual flames. 
 Boulder County provided this amazing photo early in the night from a slightly elevated vantage point.
